data.content:
# Playwright auto-approval proof note (Worker C, 2026-04-25)

Claimed state: `exploration` on lease `foolery-f574`.

## Exact local versions and package refs

- Codex: `command -v codex` -> `/Users/cartine/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.0/bin/codex`; `codex --version` -> `codex-cli 0.125.0`.
- Codex installed package refs: `/Users/cartine/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.0/lib/node_modules/@openai/codex/package.json` has version `0.125.0`; native binary at `/Users/cartine/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.0/lib/node_modules/@openai/codex/node_modules/@openai/codex-darwin-arm64/vendor/aarch64-apple-darwin/codex/codex`.
- OpenCode: `command -v opencode` -> `/opt/homebrew/bin/opencode`; `opencode --version` -> `1.14.24`; installed binary at `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/opencode/1.14.24/bin/opencode`.
- Playwright MCP: `npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --version` -> `Version 0.0.70`. Local package ref: `/Users/cartine/.npm/_npx/9833c18b2d85bc59/node_modules/@playwright/mcp/package.json` has `"version": "0.0.70"`, bin `playwright-mcp`, and deps `playwright`/`playwright-core` `1.60.0-alpha-1774999321000`.
- Knots: `kno --version` -> `kno 0.15.5`.

## Primary-source/config evidence

- Codex CLI help: `codex exec --help` documents `--ask-for-approval never` as never asking for user approval, and `--json` JSONL events. `codex mcp add --help` documents stdio MCP config.
- Codex resolved MCP config: `codex mcp get playwright` printed:
  `command: npx`, `args: -y @playwright/mcp@latest --isolated`, `default_tools_approval_mode: approve`.
- Codex global config source: `/Users/cartine/.codex/config.toml` contains `[mcp_servers.playwright]`, `args = ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@latest", "--isolated"]`, `default_tools_approval_mode = "approve"`, plus explicit approve entries for `browser_navigate`, `browser_run_code`, and `browser_click`.
- OpenCode CLI help: `opencode run --help` documents `--format json` and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` as auto-approving permissions not explicitly denied.
- OpenCode installed run handler source, extracted from `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/opencode/1.14.24/bin/opencode` with `strings`, contains the permission handler behavior: on `permission.asked`, if `D["dangerously-skip-permissions"]` it replies `once`; otherwise it prints `permission requested: ...; auto-rejecting` and replies `reject`.
- OpenCode official schema/docs: `https://opencode.ai/config.json` allows arbitrary `permission` keys with `ask|allow|deny`; `https://opencode.ai/docs/config/` documents `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT` as highest normal inline config and confirms project/global/inline config precedence.
- OpenCode resolved config with inline proof: `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT='{"permission":{"playwright_*":"allow"}}' opencode debug config` showed the final permission map includes both existing `playwright: allow` and added `playwright_*: allow`.
- OpenCode MCP connectivity: `opencode mcp list --print-logs --log-level DEBUG` showed `service=mcp key=playwright toolCount=21 create() successfully created client` and the server command `npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --isolated`.
- Playwright MCP local README lists generated tools, including `browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_run_code`, etc., under `/Users/cartine/.npm/_npx/9833c18b2d85bc59/node_modules/@playwright/mcp/README.md`.

## Negative proof: OpenCode `permission.playwright = allow` is insufficient

Command run without `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, using existing `/Users/cartine/.config/opencode/opencode.json` where `permission.playwright = "allow"`:

`opencode run --format json --print-logs --log-level DEBUG --dir /Users/cartine/foolery 'Use the Playwright MCP server exactly once. Call browser_navigate ...'`

Relevant output excerpts:

- `service=permission permission=playwright_browser_navigate pattern=* ... evaluate`
- `service=permission ... action={"permission":"*","action":"ask","pattern":"*"} evaluated`
- `permission requested: playwright_browser_navigate (*); auto-rejecting`
- JSON tool event ended with `"status":"error"` and `"error":"The user rejected permission to use this specific tool call."`

Conclusion: bare `"playwright": "allow"` does not approve the actual generated OpenCode permission `playwright_browser_navigate`. Do not recommend it as a full Playwright auto-approval config.

## Positive proof: scoped configs and automated harness

A local HTTP server served a title not present in the prompts. The harness fails if the output lacks a Playwright tool event, lacks the observed title, or contains approval/permission/rejection/error markers.

Core Codex command used by the passing harness:

`codex exec --json -C /Users/cartine/foolery -c 'approval_policy="never"' -c 'sandbox_mode="danger-full-access"' -c 'mcp_servers.playwright.command="npx"' -c 'mcp_servers.playwright.args=["-y","@playwright/mcp@0.0.70","--isolated","--headless"]' -c 'mcp_servers.playwright.default_tools_approval_mode="approve"' '<prompt to call browser_navigate to local URL>'`

Core OpenCode command used by the passing scoped harness:

`OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT='{"permission":{"playwright_*":"allow"}}' opencode run --format json --dir /Users/cartine/foolery '<prompt to call browser_navigate to local URL>'`

Final self-checking harness result:

`PASS scoped codex+opencode playwright auto-approval harness title=b7c0-scoped-harness-title-1777104115`

Representative Codex JSON excerpts from the live-page run:

- `"type":"mcp_tool_call","server":"playwright","tool":"browser_navigate","arguments":{"url":"http://127.0.0.1:18765/"}`
- result contained `Page Title: b7c0-live-title-9f4a2d`
- final agent message: `CODEX_PLAYWRIGHT_DONE b7c0-live-title-9f4a2d`

Representative OpenCode JSON excerpts from the scoped wildcard run:

- `"type":"tool_use","tool":"playwright_browser_navigate","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"url":"http://127.0.0.1:18768/"}`
- output contained `Page Title: b7c0-opencode-wildcard-title-1777104062`
- final text: `OPENCODE_PLAYWRIGHT_DONE b7c0-opencode-wildcard-title-1777104062`

## Final scoped recommendation

For the tested transport/server/version scope, use:

- Codex 0.125.0: stdio MCP server named `playwright`, command `npx -y @playwright/mcp@0.0.70 --isolated --headless`, with `mcp_servers.playwright.default_tools_approval_mode = "approve"`; for noninteractive harnesses also use `approval_policy="never"` to prevent shell-command approvals from entering the proof surface.
- OpenCode 1.14.24: stdio MCP server key `playwright`, command `npx -y @playwright/mcp@0.0.70 --isolated --headless`, plus permission rule `"playwright_*": "allow"`. This is scoped to OpenCode's observed/generated Playwright MCP tool permission names such as `playwright_browser_navigate`. It is better scoped than `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.

Do not claim or recommend OpenCode `"playwright": "allow"` alone; it was proven insufficient. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` also works for the exercised request, but it is broader than Playwright and should only be used inside an external sandbox if that broad policy is acceptable.

Missing proof: the harness exercised `browser_navigate` only. The wildcard recommendation is based on OpenCode's observed prefix permission name for that MCP server plus schema-supported wildcard permission matching; it was not exhaustively executed against every Playwright MCP tool in package `0.0.70`.

Related follow-up knot created and linked: `foolery-7420` (Codify Playwright auto-approval harness).
